lines: I am Linnet Fernwise, mender of fern-lamps and little road courtesies. These glowcaps are not stars, but they do their best.
When the lower track turns shy, count two fern-lamps, then walk where the moss is brightest.
Two fern-lamps, bright moss, then east. If a road gives you a rhyme, it is only being polite.
Mind the cart wheel. It squeaks in Old Speech and refuses to translate for strangers.
